lesson: Visible interface work must be verified by the owner of visible behavior, not inferred from non-visual checks.
trigger: A task passed build checks but still needed visual verification before completion.
future_behavior_change: Before marking visible UI work done, request or run owner-appropriate visual evidence and attach the artifact path.

verification.result: The harness rule now distinguishes non-visual verification from visual ownership.

loop_trace.recall: Prior routing memory identified visible UI as designer-owned.
loop_trace.action: The implementation owner completed the visible work and produced artifact paths.
loop_trace.verification: The result was accepted only after visual evidence existed.
loop_trace.learning: Future visible UI completion requires visual evidence, not only build evidence.
